observations.title: Test timing patterns: rolling window drift and subprocess timeout cascades
observations.content:
Two test failure patterns discovered in CI:

1. **Rolling window timestamp drift** — Tests with hardcoded dates (e.g., "2026-03-10") silently break when real time advances past the rolling window boundary (7-day or 4-hour windows). Fix: use `new Date(Date.now() - 60*60*1000).toISOString()` for timestamps that must stay within the window.

2. **Subprocess timeout cascade** — bun:test defaults to 5s per test. Quality-check tests spawning tsc/eslint/prettier can take 5-30s in CI. When first test times out, the sidecar's concurrency guard (`activeChecks.has(projectPath)`) still has the project locked, causing ALL subsequent tests to fail with "already running". Fix: add per-test timeouts via third argument to `it()`: `it("name", async () => {...}, 60_000)`.

Affected files: `src/sessions/usage-stats.test.ts`, `src/sidecar/quality-routes.test.ts`, `src/sidecar/client.test.ts`. Skill: `.claude/skills/sentinal-test-timing/SKILL.md`
observations.author: Evan Tiley

observations.title: Platform hook architecture: MCP (~5-20ms) vs Claude Code hooks (~50-200ms) vs OpenCode plugin (~1-5ms)
observations.content:
Decision framework for new sidecar-dependent features:

**Performance ranking:**
1. OpenCode plugin handler: ~1-5ms (in-process, single SidecarClient via closure)
2. MCP tool (both platforms): ~5-20ms (long-running process, single client at startup)
3. Claude Code hooks: ~50-200ms (NEW process per event, fresh connect each time)

**When to use each:**
- MCP tool: on-demand queries, user/AI-initiated operations
- Claude Code hook: must intercept/block tool calls (PreToolUse), or react to lifecycle events (SessionStart, PreCompact, UserPromptSubmit)
- OpenCode plugin: all event-reactive logic (tool.execute.before/after, event, session.compacting)

**Key gotchas:**
- OpenCode `tool.execute.after` `input.tool` uses built-in names ("write","edit","bash") — MCP tool calls have different names and won't match `MEMORY_TOOLS` filter
- Claude Code async hooks CAN return additionalContext but CANNOT return decision/block
- OpenCode `client.app.log()` writes to TUI only, NOT LLM context — use compact-state.json
- Claude Code subagent frontmatter supports `mcpServers` for scoping; OpenCode does not

Skill: `.claude/skills/sentinal-hook-architecture/SKILL.md`
observations.author: Evan Tiley
